title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City
notes: Unhandled exception

title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City
notes:
Works fine ✓ but:
- You have to press [Alt]+[Enter] when it launches, otherwise it's stuck as transparent window and soon after this it crashes,
- After this it works fine at fullscreen (set it to 1920x1080 in options),
- Don't remember if it's a Linux thing but you can only change *horizontal* mouse sensitivity, while vertical stays very sensitive, so you might want to keep it default and e.g. instead switch to lower DPI on your mouse,
- Steam Overlay and screenshots work out of the box,
- Otherwise: game saves work and it works smoothly with "single click"!

title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City
notes:
You sometimes need to manually change focus to the Wine window, to play the game. 

Played for 15 minutes, seems stable enough. Sounds work, music works. Controls work fine. Didn't test using a controller.

title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Works very well. Saving the game doesn't work out of the box. You must create a folder called My Documents In (/home/username/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/gameID/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Runs perfectly. If your computer appears to lock up or you get 'unhandled exception error' just hit ALT + ENTER and works like nothing is wrong.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

gpu: Intel Open Source Technology Center Mesa DRI Intel(R) Sandybridge Mobile x86/MMX/SSE2
notes: The intro movies do not work, you can rename the files, and press enter to skip. After that It works fine, even resolution changes work (on windows 10 does not).
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes:
It shows error after start. Just open the game with pressed alt+enter.
I don't have any others errors/freezes in game.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Managed to complete the game with no crashes or graphical issues
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: played without crashes or anything
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: game runs fine after pressing enter after start (intros don't work). widescreenfix and silentpatch also work without problems.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Finished the game, works almost flawlessly, except for the intro which doesn't run and mouse support, which sometimes requires an alt+tab to work properly
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Upon launching, you need to hit Alt + Tab to show the game screen and give you mouse control. Works perfectly. 30fps on this old hardware
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: No graphical and performance problems. Game runs as native.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Turning ON the frame limiter gets rid of a couple of annoying bugs (in the options menu), but otherwise runs perfectly.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: encountered a mouse movement bug when camera keeps spinning leftwards and towards bottom without any discernible reason. so far this is the only bug that prevents the game from being playable
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: the startup game did not open, it was a black screen and opened a window with "unhandled exception", searching I found that it was just press Alt + Enter and it was solved, I was able to put the maximum resolution 1920x1080, all the controls working, audio working correctly
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Character is stuck circling down and left, otherwise everything seems to be working fine out of the box. Cannot play like this however
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: First trouble with an exception, after pressing alt + enter it works like a charm.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City

notes: Working perfectly, the first time was giving black screen but after adding the command "PROTON_NO_D3D11 = 1% command%" at startup the problem was solved, I noticed some momentary crashes on the first mission. it is probably "rendering" failure. I played around 1:50 am and I did not notice any other problems.
